reasoning: Given the current observation, we can see that voltage (V) is directly proportional to resistance (R) and inversely proportional to the current (I). We can start by testing the relationship between voltage and resistance.

The current observation suggests that the relationship between voltage and resistance holds true, as the voltage doubled when the resistance was doubled while the current remained constant. Now, we will test the relationship between voltage and current.

The current observation suggests that the relationship between voltage and resistance (R) and current (I) follows Ohm's Law (V = I * R). We have tested the relationship between voltage and resistance, and now we have tested the relationship between voltage and current. We can now combine our findings to form the final equation.
